brand.name: AlgoViz Pro
brand.identity: The Engineering Standard for Algorithm Mastery

headings.usage: Landing page headers, Dashboard section titles, Modal headers

code.usage: Code editor, complexity notation (O(n)), algorithm steps

light.description: Clean, academic, paper-like. Focus on high contrast for daylight reading.

dark.description: Standard IDE theme. Deep blues/grays. Reduces eye strain.

hacker.description: Matrix/Terminal style. High contrast neon green on pure black. Monospace everything preferred.

zones.sidebar: col-span-2 hidden lg:block h-[calc(100vh-4rem)] sticky top-16
zones.canvas_area: col-span-12 lg:col-span-7 h-[600px] lg:h-auto min-h-[500px] rounded-xl border bg-card relative overflow-hidden
zones.code_panel: col-span-12 lg:col-span-3 h-[300px] lg:h-auto bg-muted/30 rounded-xl border p-4
zones.analytics_bar: col-span-12 h-20 bg-card border rounded-lg flex items-center justify-between px-6

buttons.primary: rounded-full font-medium shadow-lg hover:shadow-xl transition-all duration-300 active:scale-95
buttons.controls: h-10 w-10 rounded-full p-2 hover:bg-accent flex items-center justify-center transition-transform hover:scale-110

cards.default: rounded-xl border bg-card text-card-foreground shadow-sm hover:shadow-md transition-shadow duration-300

inputs.default: h-10 rounded-md border border-input bg-transparent px-3 py-1 text-sm shadow-sm transition-colors file:border-0 file:bg-transparent file:text-sm file:font-medium placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-1 focus-visible:ring-ring disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50
inputs.hacker: rounded-none border-primary bg-black text-primary placeholder:text-primary/50 focus:ring-primary

borders.tracing_beam: use framer-motion to animate border color

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. THEME SETUP: Modify `index.css` to include the 'hacker' class definition alongside 'dark' and ':root'. Use the color values provided in `colors.palettes.hacker`.
2. FONT SETUP: Import 'Space Grotesk', 'Manrope', and 'JetBrains Mono' from Google Fonts in `index.css`.

3. LAYOUT: For the main Visualizer page, use a Resizable Panel Layout (using `react-resizable-panels` or similar, or just Flex/Grid if simpler) to allow users to adjust the size of the Canvas vs Code vs Controls.
4. VISUALIZATION CANVAS: This is the 'Hero' of the app. It must have a subtle grid background (`bg-grid-white/[0.02]`) and be centered.
5. CONTROLS: Group controls logically (Playback, Speed, Input Size). Do not scatter them. Use a 'Control Bar' at the bottom or a 'Control Panel' on the side.
6. AI TUTOR: Make it a collapsible sidebar or a floating drawer. It should not block the main view.
7. DEBUGGER: Use a tabular view for 'Distance Arrays' or 'Visited Nodes'. Make it look like a real debugger watch window.
8. ACCESSIBILITY: Ensure color contrast ratios are met, especially in 'Hacker Mode'. Use text labels alongside icons.
9. CHARTING: Use `recharts` for the Complexity Analysis charts. Style them to match the active theme (Grid lines, Axis colors).
universal_guidelines: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }` in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
